title: An autonomous laboratory for the accelerated synthesis of inorganic materials
project_lesson: Closed loops need computation, literature-derived priors, ML/active learning, robotic execution, and failure analysis as separate auditable layers.

title: Autonomous chemical research with large language models
project_lesson: Autonomous experiment planning must expose tool use, planning, execution, and human-auditable steps; do not hide experimental decisions inside one opaque LLM call.

title: Augmenting large language models with chemistry tools
project_lesson: Domain tools should be explicit modules behind an LLM planner; CultivateAgent should keep deterministic normalization/evidence tools outside the LLM.

title: The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery
project_lesson: Useful for staged ideation/execution/review structure, but too autonomous for wet-lab claims; keep human gates before experiments.

title: Language agents achieve superhuman synthesis of scientific knowledge
project_lesson: Use full-text retrieval, citation grounding, query refinement, and contradiction checks; abstract-only extraction is insufficient.

title: Synthesizing scientific literature with retrieval-augmented language models
project_lesson: Specialized scientific RAG benefits from large paper stores, adaptive retrieval, and self-feedback; evidence citation accuracy should be measured.

title: LAB-Bench: Language Agent Biology Benchmark
project_lesson: Use biology-specific evaluation tasks and report accuracy/coverage; CultivateAgent should benchmark extraction/review tasks, not only demo outputs.

title: Structured information extraction from scientific text with large language models
project_lesson: Structured JSON-style extraction and task-specific evaluation are appropriate; broad schemas should be decomposed into field/section-specific jobs.

title: Large Language Models for Scientific Information Extraction: An Empirical Study for Virology
project_lesson: Scientific IE can be framed as structured contribution summaries; smaller fine-tuned models may compete on narrow IE after enough annotations.

title: GROBID: a machine learning software for extracting information from scholarly documents
project_lesson: PDFs should be converted into structured TEI/section/table objects where possible; plain fulltext.txt is too weak for reliable extraction.

title: S2ORC: The Semantic Scholar Open Research Corpus
project_lesson: Structured full text with citations, figures, and tables linked to paper objects is the target data model to emulate locally.

title: DocETL: Agentic Query Rewriting and Evaluation for Complex Document Processing
project_lesson: LLM document processing should be modular, schema-bound, and evaluated operator-by-operator rather than one monolithic prompt.

title: Scalable Global Optimization via Local Bayesian Optimization
project_lesson: Trust-region BO is a later upgrade when medium search space grows high-dimensional; not the immediate S3 bottleneck.

title: Scalable Constrained Bayesian Optimization
project_lesson: Useful later for hard constraints such as cost, animal-origin, osmolality, or food-grade bounds.

title: Joint Entropy Search for Multi-objective Bayesian Optimization
project_lesson: Information-theoretic MOBO is a later candidate when learning the Pareto set matters more than short-term hypervolume.

title: Diversified Sampling for Batched Bayesian Optimization with Determinantal Point Processes
project_lesson: Batch diversity is useful once wet-lab batches risk near-duplicate formulations.

title: Understanding the output (TEI)
project_lesson: GROBID uses TEI XML for structured document extraction; full-text output includes body div/head/p structures and figures, with tables commonly represented as figure type table.

title: GROBID Service API
project_lesson: GROBID recommends the web-service mode for production and benchmarking; CultivateAgent should call processFulltextDocument optionally, save returned TEI, and keep the plain-text fallback when the service is unavailable.

title: SchemaRAG: Dynamic Large Schema Reduction for LLM-driven Structured Information Extraction
project_lesson: Large fixed schemas hurt LLM IE; reduce/route the schema per document. Directly motivates decomposing the A-M schema into small per-document operator subsets rather than one monolithic prompt.

title: Effective and Efficient Schema-aware Information Extraction Using On-Device Large Language Models
project_lesson: Smaller focused schema contexts improve IE reliability; supports operator decomposition and section routing.

title: Meta-analysis in clinical trials (DerSimonian-Laird random-effects)
project_lesson: Closed-form random-effects pooling of heterogeneous effect sizes with between-study variance tau^2. Core method for the cross-paper evidence layer (no MCMC).

title: Quantifying heterogeneity in a meta-analysis (I^2)
project_lesson: I^2 quantifies between-study heterogeneity; used to flag context-dependent components that need direct experiments rather than pooled belief.

title: Weakly informative priors for the heterogeneity parameter in Bayesian random-effects meta-analysis
project_lesson: Guides the tau (heterogeneity) prior for a Bayesian random-effects variant; informs uncertainty widening under sparse/conflicting evidence.

title: Multi-objective Bayesian algorithm automatically discovers low-cost high-growth serum-free media for cellular agriculture
project_lesson: DIRECT PRIOR ART: MOBO already discovers serum-free media (C2C12). CultivateAgent must differentiate: literature-grounded space+priors, LLM evidence-cited proposer, and traceability vs pure BO.

title: piBO: Augmenting Acquisition Functions with User Beliefs for Bayesian Optimization
project_lesson: Mechanism for injecting a prior over promising regions into the acquisition, decaying as data accrues. CultivateAgent uses it to fold evidence-synthesis posteriors into the MOBO.

title: GRADE criteria for assessing certainty of evidence
venue_year: GRADE Book / CDC ACIP GRADE handbook
project_lesson: Evidence-to-action gates should explicitly flag inconsistency, indirectness, and imprecision; CultivateAgent adapts this conservatively for wet-lab entry rather than making clinical-style recommendations.

title: PRISMA 2020 statement: updated guideline for reporting systematic reviews
project_lesson: Systematic evidence records should separate source identification, screening, synthesis, and reporting; supports keeping corpus manifest, review queue, evidence audit, and design packet distinct.

title: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework 1.0
project_lesson: AI-assisted scientific decisions need mapped context, measured validity, transparent records, and managed risk; supports a deterministic no-go audit before wet-lab action.

title: ASReview: open source machine learning framework for efficient and transparent systematic reviews
project_lesson: Human-in-the-loop review tools should prioritize likely relevant records while keeping the process transparent; supports review-packet generation without AI adjudication.

title: SWIFT-Review: a text-mining workbench for systematic review
project_lesson: Systematic review experts should remain in control while software prioritizes and organizes evidence; supports locator packets for H001-H016.

title: RobotReviewer: automated extraction and risk-of-bias support for clinical trials
project_lesson: Automated systems can surface supporting text but should not replace human judgment; supports character-range locators rather than AI evidence decisions.

title: PaperMage: A Unified Toolkit for Processing, Representing, and Manipulating Visually-Rich Scientific Documents
project_lesson: Scientific PDF pipelines should expose sections, paragraphs, tables, and figures as explicit document objects; supports operator-readiness checks before LLM extraction.

title: Nougat: Neural Optical Understanding for Academic Documents
project_lesson: PDF-to-markup parsers can recover machine-readable scientific text when normal PDF text is weak; supports keeping parser alternatives/fallbacks distinct from evidence claims.

title: Journal Article Tag Suite Publishing Tag Library: article, body/sec, and table-wrap
project_lesson: JATS encodes article full text with article/front/body/back, body sections, titles, paragraphs, and table-wrap tables; supports direct parser support for Europe PMC-style fulltext.xml files.

title: Europe PMC Articles RESTful API fullTextXML
project_lesson: Europe PMC fullTextXML retrieves Open Access full text XML; local fulltext.xml files may be JATS rather than GROBID TEI and should be auto-detected before extraction readiness.

title: DeepSeek API Docs: Your First API Call and Models & Pricing
project_lesson: DeepSeek exposes OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic-compatible API formats; current model names include deepseek-v4-flash and deepseek-v4-pro, while deepseek-chat/deepseek-reasoner are compatibility names marked deprecated after 2026-07-24 15:59 UTC; low-cost live outputs still require grounding and human review.

title: DeepSeek API Docs: Error Codes
project_lesson: DeepSeek classifies 401 authentication, 402 balance, 400 invalid format, and 422 invalid parameter as configuration/account/request issues, while 429/500/503 are rate-limit/server conditions; supports fail-fast for non-retryable provider errors before live operator extraction.

title: Cochrane MECIR standards for selecting studies and collecting data
project_lesson: Study selection and outcome-data extraction should use predefined forms, transparent decisions, duplicate or independent checking where feasible, and explicit disagreement resolution; supports keeping S4 human decisions separate from AI-generated locators.

title: Transparent Reporting of AI in Systematic Literature Reviews: Development of the PRISMA-trAIce Checklist
project_lesson: AI-assisted literature review reports should document the AI tool, human-AI interaction, manual verification proportion, performance evaluation, and limitations; supports logging provider/model/mode and human validation status for S3-S4.

title: Toward systematic review automation: a practical guide to using machine learning tools in research synthesis
project_lesson: Systematic-review automation is best treated as semi-automation: models can rank records and extract candidate snippets, but humans stay in the loop and stopping/exclusion decisions must remain conservative.

title: Cochrane Handbook Chapter 6: Choosing effect measures and computing estimates of effect
project_lesson: Continuous outcomes may use mean differences, standardized mean differences, or ratio measures; ratio measures are analyzed on the logarithmic scale. Supports quote-based log fold-change only when the source reports an explicit ratio or percent change.

title: The meta-analysis of response ratios in experimental ecology
project_lesson: The log response ratio is an established effect-size statistic for proportional experimental responses; supports deterministic ln(fold-change) extraction when a quote explicitly reports fold/percent change.

title: Ratio of means for analyzing continuous outcomes in meta-analysis performed as well as mean difference methods
project_lesson: Ratio of means compares the experimental mean with the control mean and is analyzed as a ratio measure; supports deterministic ln(treatment_mean/control_mean) extraction only when both means are explicitly reported in a verified quote.

title: metafor escalc documentation for measure ROM
project_lesson: The ROM measure is log(m1i/m2i), requires group means, SDs, and sample sizes, and its default sampling variance uses the large-sample approximation from Hedges et al. 1999. Supports deterministic variance only when mean, SD or SE, and n are all quoted.

title: Cochrane Handbook Chapter 5: Collecting data
project_lesson: Systematic reviews should predefine and pilot structured collection forms, collect sufficient unambiguous outcome and intervention data, expose missing items, and independently check decision-critical outcome data. Supports per-concept Gate 2 coverage instead of relying on a pooled completeness average.
